title: CONSTRUCTS Act of 2025
stage_label: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.
plain_summary: The CONSTRUCTS Act of 2025 creates a new program where the government will give money to community colleges and trade schools to help them teach people how to build houses. The program will especially help schools in rural areas and places that don't have as many resources. The government plans to spend twenty million dollars each year on this program from 2025 to 2029. This will help train more people for construction jobs so they can build homes.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions.

sections.title: Section 1. Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Creating Opportunities for New Skills Training at Rural or Underserved Colleges and Trade Schools Act of two thousand twenty five. It can also be called the CONSTRUCTS Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2. Education and Training for Careers in Residential Construction
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act. It adds a new section called Education and Training for Careers in Residential Construction. The new section defines important terms. An incumbent worker means someone who already has a job and wants to improve their skills. A junior or community college is a two year college. A rural area means places that are not in big cities or metropolitan areas. An underserved population means groups of people who do not have as many job opportunities in construction as other groups, including people with low incomes, people who face barriers to getting jobs, and veterans. The Secretary of Labor will work with the Secretary of Education to create a program that gives grants to schools and training providers. These grants will help them expand their ability to provide training, education, and outreach for jobs in home building. Each grant can last up to four years. Schools that can get grants include community colleges, career and technical education schools, and approved training providers. To get a grant, schools must submit an application that describes their training plans, the people they will serve, their partnerships with construction businesses, expected outcomes for students, and how the training will help increase affordable housing in their area. The Secretary of Labor will give priority to schools that serve rural areas or underserved populations. Schools that get grants must use the money to create or expand training programs for construction skills like carpentry, plumbing, electrical work, and construction management. They must also create partnerships with local construction businesses and do outreach to elementary and high school students about construction careers. Schools can also use grant money to hire experienced instructors, operate training clinics in underserved areas, create promotional materials, and provide scholarships to students. Schools must offer flexible schedules including night classes, part time schedules, and online courses to help people who work during the day or live in rural areas. They must also help graduates find jobs after completing their training. Any organization that gets grant money must promise they follow all federal, state, and local labor laws and are not facing any legal action for breaking these laws. Schools must report their results to the Secretary of Labor every year starting eighteen months after getting their grant. The Secretary of Labor must then report these results to Congress. Twenty million dollars is authorized to be spent on this program each year from two thousand twenty five through two thousand twenty nine.
